original_abstract:
The term "Metaverse" has rapidly moved into the mainstream, driven by massive investments from technology conglomerates and a surging interest in Web 3.0, blockchain, NFTs, and cryptocurrencies. Despite this momentum, the scientific community lacks a definitive consensus on the Metaverse's conceptual boundaries. This paper addresses this gap by collecting, analyzing, and synthesizing existing scientific definitions and their core characteristics through a Systematic Literature Review (SLR). 

Building on this synthesis, we present two revised definitions that condense the key attributes of the Metaverse. The first offers a simplistic, holistic view, describing it as "a three-dimensional online environment in which users, represented by avatars, interact within virtual spaces decoupled from the physical world." The second definition provides a more technical and detailed specification, which we explore in depth. These definitions serve as a foundational framework for scholars in systems science, information systems, computer science, and business informatics. Furthermore, the paper outlines open research challenges, explores the social, economic, and technical implications of a unified Metaverse, and discusses the critical preconditions necessary for its successful implementation.
humanized_abstract:
All of a sudden the word “metaverse” has become a popular term in the media and technology circles because of the heavy investments made by technology giants into this new trend, as well as the increasing popularity of concepts such as Web 3.0, blockchain, NFTs and cryptocurrencies. Although many technological entities have explored and are constantly developing the idea of the metaverse, there is no scientific consensus around it. Thus, this research work aims at a first step towards a better understanding of the term. Using a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) approach, this paper identifies, analyzes and compiles scientific definitions, attributes and components that describe what the metaverse is.

Based on the above synthesis, we define two reduced sets of Metaverse characteristics: The first one defines the Metaverse in a very simple and comprehensive way: The Metaverse is a three-dimensional online world where users, represented by avatars, can interact in a virtual space which is completely separate from real life. The second one is more technical and is detailed in the following subsection. Our goal is to provide a solid basis for future research by scholars in the areas of systems science, information systems, computer science and business informatics. We conclude this paper by highlighting open research questions, discussing the social, economic and technological consequences of a unifying Metaverse, and by outlining the preconditions for its successful implementation.
